teaser: Born at UK music festivals in 2007, Chapati Man is the authentic Indian street food brand that's already found its way onto the shelves of Morrisons and Waitrose — and into the hearts of festival-goers, Londoners, and food lovers across the UK. With a flagship store and Central Production Unit now open in Whitechapel, East London, and a proven grab-and-go model built for high-footfall kiosks, Chapati Man is ready to roll out with franchise partners who want to ride the booming demand for Indian, Asian and Middle Eastern food in every major UK city.
investmentDisplay: £20,000 – £25,000 franchise fee (fit-out variable by unit size)
locationsDisplay: London, Manchester, Leeds, Birmingham, Edinburgh & more
highlights: Authentic Indian street food with a 17-year heritage — festival roots, Morrisons & Waitrose listings, flagship store in Whitechapel E1
£2,500–£3,000 daily revenue potential | two-staff kiosk model | 6–8% royalty + 3–5% marketing fee
Multi-unit growth model targeting 3–5 stores across London and major UK cities — perfectly timed for the surge in Indian food demand
